preview_text:
The model projects Spain to win with 90% probability, reflecting a commanding advantage over Saudi Arabia. The underdog is assigned essentially zero chance of victory in the statistical forecast, a stark asymmetry that signals the model sees a heavily lopsided contest.

The draw emerges as the only plausible alternative outcome in the model's view, at nearly 10%. Spain's win probability so dominates the spread that the match is expected to be competitive only in the narrow sense that a stalemate remains statistically possible; Saudi Arabia carries no meaningful path to three points.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge meaningfully on the underdog. The market assigns Saudi Arabia 7 percentage points—a non-trivial gap that reflects modest, if small, credence in an upset. The market also lowers Spain's win probability to 83%, a 7-point gap favoring the underdog relative to the model. Both sources agree draws are rare. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source better captures true match dynamics.
